name: RhinoApp
summary: .NET RhinoApp is parallel to C++ CRhinoApp.

methods.name: AskUserForRhinoLicense
methods.signature: public static bool AskUserForRhinoLicense(bool standAlone, Object parentWindow)
methods.summary: Display UI asking the user to enter a license for Rhino or use one from the Zoo.

params.name: standAlone
params.doc: True to ask for a stand-alone license, false to ask the user for a license from the Zoo

params.name: parentWindow
params.doc: Parent window for the user interface dialog.

methods.name: CapturedCommandWindowStrings
methods.summary: Get list of strings sent to the command window through calls to Write or WriteLine when capturing has been enabled

params.name: clearBuffer
params.doc: Clear the captured buffer after this call

methods.name: ChangeLicenseKey
methods.summary: Display UI asking the user to enter a license for the product specified by licenseId.

params.name: pluginId
params.doc: Guid identifying the plug-in that is requesting a change of license key

methods.name: ClearCommandHistoryWindow
methods.summary: Clear the text in Rhino's command history window.

methods.name: ExecuteCommand
methods.signature: public static Result ExecuteCommand(RhinoDoc document, string commandName)
methods.summary: Execute a Rhino command.

params.name: document
params.doc: Document to execute the command for

params.name: commandName
params.doc: Name of command to run. Use command's localized name or preface with an underscore.

returns.doc: Returns the result of the command.

methods.name: Exit()
methods.summary: Exits, or closes, Rhino.

methods.name: Exit(Boolean)
methods.summary: Exits, or forcefully closes Rhino. A prompt to allow saving will appear if necessary when forcefully exiting Works on Windows and MacOS true to allow the user to cancel exiting false to force exit

methods.name: GetDataDirectory(Boolean, Boolean)
methods.signature: public static string GetDataDirectory(bool localUser, bool forceDirectoryCreation)
methods.summary: Gets the data directory.

params.name: localUser
params.doc: If set to true local user.

params.name: forceDirectoryCreation
params.doc: If set to true force directory creation.

methods.name: GetDataDirectory(Boolean, Boolean, String)
methods.signature: public static string GetDataDirectory(bool localUser, bool forceDirectoryCreation, string subDirectory)
methods.summary: Gets the data directory.

params.name: localUser
params.doc: If set to true local user.

params.name: forceDirectoryCreation
params.doc: If set to true force directory creation.

params.name: subDirectory
params.doc: Sub directory, will get appended to the end of the data directory. if forceDirectoryCreation is true then this directory will get created and writable.

methods.name: GetExecutableDirectory
methods.summary: directory

methods.name: GetPlugInObject(Guid)
methods.summary: Gets the object that is returned by PlugIn.GetPlugInObject for a given plug-in. This function attempts to find and load a plug-in with a given Id. When a plug-in is found, it's GetPlugInObject function is called and the result is returned here. Note the plug-in must have already been installed in Rhino or the plug-in manager will not know where to look for a plug-in with a matching id.

returns.doc: Result of PlugIn.GetPlugInObject for a given plug-in on success.

methods.name: GetPlugInObject(String)
methods.summary: Gets the object that is returned by PlugIn.GetPlugInObject for a given plug-in. This function attempts to find and load a plug-in with a given name. When a plug-in is found, it's GetPlugInObject function is called and the result is returned here. Note the plug-in must have already been installed in Rhino or the plug-in manager will not know where to look for a plug-in with a matching name.

returns.doc: Result of PlugIn.GetPlugInObject for a given plug-in on success.

methods.name: InFullScreen
methods.summary: Verifies that Rhino is running in full screen mode.

returns.doc: true if Rhino is running full screen, false otherwise.

methods.name: InvokeAndWait
methods.summary: Work-In-Progress. Testing this with our unit test framework

methods.name: InvokeOnUiThread
methods.signature: public static void InvokeOnUiThread(Delegate method, params Object[] args)
methods.summary: Execute a function on the main UI thread.

params.name: args
params.doc: parameters to pass to the function

methods.name: IsInstallationBeta
methods.summary: If licenseType is a beta license, returns true. A beta license grants full use of the product during the pre-release development period.

returns.doc: true if licenseType is a beta license. false otherwise

methods.name: IsInstallationCommercial
methods.summary: If licenseType is a commercial license, returns true. A commercial license grants full use of the product.

returns.doc: true if licenseType is a commercial license. false otherwise

methods.name: IsInstallationEvaluation
methods.summary: If licenseType is an evaluation license, returns true. An evaluation license limits the ability of Rhino to save based on either the number of saves or a fixed period of time.

returns.doc: true if licenseType is an evaluation license. false otherwise

methods.name: LoginToCloudZoo
methods.summary: Logs in to the cloud zoo.

methods.name: MainWindowHandle
methods.summary: Gets the HWND of the Rhino main window.

methods.name: OutputDebugString
methods.summary: Print a string to the Visual Studio Output window, if a debugger is attached. Note that the developer needs to add a newline manually if the next output should come on a separate line.

params.name: str
params.doc: The string to print to the Output window.

methods.name: ParseTextField(String, RhinoObject, RhinoObject)
methods.signature: public static string ParseTextField(string formula, RhinoObject obj, RhinoObject topParentObject)
methods.summary: Parses a text field string.

params.name: obj
params.doc: The Rhino object. Value can be IntPtr.Zero.

params.name: topParentObject
params.doc: The parent Rhino object. Value can be IntPtr.Zero.

returns.doc: The parsed text field if sucessful.

methods.name: ParseTextField(String, RhinoObject, RhinoObject, InstanceObject)
methods.signature: public static string ParseTextField(string formula, RhinoObject obj, RhinoObject topParentObject, InstanceObject immediateParent)
methods.summary: Parses a text field string.

params.name: obj
params.doc: The Rhino object. Value can be IntPtr.Zero.

params.name: topParentObject
params.doc: The parent Rhino object. Value can be IntPtr.Zero.

params.name: immediateParent
params.doc: The immediate parent instance object. Value can be IntPtr.Zero.

returns.doc: The parsed text field if sucessful.

methods.name: PostCancelEvent
methods.summary: Post a cancel event to the command line

params.name: runtimeDocSerialNumber
params.doc: Unique serialNumber for the document to post the event to.

methods.name: PostEnterEvent
methods.signature: public static void PostEnterEvent(uint runtimeDocSerialNumber, bool bRepeatedEnter)
methods.summary: Post an enter event to the command line

params.name: runtimeDocSerialNumber
params.doc: Unique serialNumber for the document to post the event to.

params.name: bRepeatedEnter
params.doc: if true, allow multiple enter events to be posted simultaneouslyt.

methods.name: RefreshRhinoLicense
methods.summary: Refresh the license used by Rhino. This allows any part of Rhino to ensure that the most current version of the license file on disk is in use.

methods.name: ReleaseMouseCapture
methods.summary: Releases the mouse capture.

methods.name: RunMenuScript
methods.summary: Run a Rhino menu item script. Will add the selected menu string to the MRU command menu.
methods.remarks: Rhino acts as if each character in the script string had been typed in the command prompt. When RunScript is called from a "script runner" command, it completely runs the script before returning. When RunScript is called outside of a command, it returns and the script is run. This way menus and buttons can use RunScript to execute complicated functions.

throws.when: If RunScript is being called while inside an event watcher.

methods.name: RunningInRdp
methods.summary: Find out if Rhino is running in a remote session

returns.doc: true if Rhino is running in a RDP session, false otherwise

methods.name: RunningOnVMWare
methods.summary: Verifies that Rhino is running on VMWare

returns.doc: true if Rhino is running in Windows on VMWare, false otherwise

methods.name: RunScript(String, Boolean)
methods.signature: public static bool RunScript(string script, bool echo)
methods.summary: Runs a Rhino command script.
methods.remarks: Rhino acts as if each character in the script string had been typed in the command prompt. When RunScript is called from a "script runner" command, it completely runs the script before returning. When RunScript is called outside of a command, it returns and the script is run. This way menus and buttons can use RunScript to execute complicated functions.

params.name: echo
params.doc: Controls how the script is echoed in the command output window. false = silent - nothing is echoed. true = verbatim - the script is echoed literally.

throws.when: If RunScript is being called while inside an event watcher.

methods.name: RunScript(String, String, Boolean)
methods.signature: public static bool RunScript(string script, string mruDisplayString, bool echo)
methods.summary: Runs a Rhino command script.
methods.remarks: Rhino acts as if each character in the script string had been typed in the command prompt. When RunScript is called from a "script runner" command, it completely runs the script before returning. When RunScript is called outside of a command, it returns and the script is run. This way menus and buttons can use RunScript to execute complicated functions.

params.name: mruDisplayString
params.doc: [in] String to display in the most recent command list.

params.name: echo
params.doc: Controls how the script is echoed in the command output window. false = silent - nothing is echoed. true = verbatim - the script is echoed literally.

throws.when: If RunScript is being called while inside an event watcher.

methods.name: RunScript(UInt32, String, Boolean)
methods.signature: public static bool RunScript(uint documentSerialNumber, string script, bool echo)
methods.summary: Runs a Rhino command script.
methods.remarks: Rhino acts as if each character in the script string had been typed in the command prompt. When RunScript is called from a "script runner" command, it completely runs the script before returning. When RunScript is called outside of a command, it returns and the script is run. This way menus and buttons can use RunScript to execute complicated functions.

params.name: documentSerialNumber
params.doc: [in] Document serial number for the document to run the script for.

params.name: echo
params.doc: [in] Controls how the script is echoed in the command output window. false = silent - nothing is echoed. true = verbatim - the script is echoed literally.

throws.when: If RunScript is being called while inside an event watcher.

methods.name: RunScript(UInt32, String, String, Boolean)
methods.signature: public static bool RunScript(uint documentSerialNumber, string script, string mruDisplayString, bool echo)
methods.summary: Runs a Rhino command script.
methods.remarks: Rhino acts as if each character in the script string had been typed in the command prompt. When RunScript is called from a "script runner" command, it completely runs the script before returning. When RunScript is called outside of a command, it returns and the script is run. This way menus and buttons can use RunScript to execute complicated functions.

params.name: documentSerialNumber
params.doc: [in] Document serial number for the document to run the script for.

params.name: mruDisplayString
params.doc: [in] String to display in the most recent command list.

params.name: echo
params.doc: [in] Controls how the script is echoed in the command output window. false = silent - nothing is echoed. true = verbatim - the script is echoed literally.

throws.when: If RunScript is being called while inside an event watcher.

methods.name: SendKeystrokes
methods.signature: public static void SendKeystrokes(string characters, bool appendReturn)
methods.summary: Sends a string of printable characters, including spaces, to Rhino's command line.

params.name: characters
params.doc: [in] A string to characters to send to the command line. This can be null.

params.name: appendReturn
params.doc: [in] Append a return character to the end of the string.

methods.name: SetCommandPrompt(String)
methods.summary: Set Rhino command prompt.

methods.name: SetCommandPrompt(String, String)
methods.signature: public static void SetCommandPrompt(string prompt, string promptDefault)
methods.summary: Sets the command prompt in Rhino.

params.name: promptDefault
params.doc: Text that appears in angle brackets and indicates what will happen if the user pressed ENTER.

methods.name: SetCommandPromptMessage
methods.summary: Set the text that appears in the Rhino command prompt. In general, you should use the SetCommandPrompt functions. In rare cases, like worker thread messages, the message that appears in the prompt has non-standard formatting. In these rare cases, SetCommandPromptMessage can be used to literally specify the text that appears in the command prompt window.

params.name: prompt
params.doc: A literal text for the command prompt window.

methods.name: SetFocusToMainWindow()
methods.summary: Sets the focus to the main window. This function attempts to use the ActiveDoc on Mac to figure out which window to set focus to.

methods.name: SetFocusToMainWindow(RhinoDoc)
methods.summary: Sets the focus to the main windows for a given document

params.name: doc
params.doc: the document to use for determining a "main window"

methods.name: Wait
methods.summary: Pauses to keep Windows message pump alive so views will update and windows will repaint.

methods.name: Write(String)
methods.summary: Print formatted text in the command window.

methods.name: Write(String, Object)
methods.signature: public static void Write(string format, Object arg0)
methods.summary: Print formatted text in the command window.

methods.name: Write(String, Object, Object)
methods.signature: public static void Write(string format, Object arg0, Object arg1)
methods.summary: Print formatted text in the command window.

methods.name: Write(String, Object, Object, Object)
methods.signature: public static void Write(string format, Object arg0, Object arg1, Object arg2)
methods.summary: Print formatted text in the command window.

methods.name: WriteLine()
methods.summary: Print a newline in the command window.

methods.name: WriteLine(String)
methods.summary: Print text in the command window.

methods.name: WriteLine(String, Object)
methods.signature: public static void WriteLine(string format, Object arg0)
methods.summary: Print formatted text with a newline in the command window.

methods.name: WriteLine(String, Object, Object)
methods.signature: public static void WriteLine(string format, Object arg0, Object arg1)
methods.summary: Print formatted text with a newline in the command window.

methods.name: WriteLine(String, Object, Object, Object)
methods.signature: public static void WriteLine(string format, Object arg0, Object arg1, Object arg2)
methods.summary: Print formatted text with a newline in the command window.

properties.name: BuildDate
properties.summary: Gets the build date.

properties.name: CanSave
properties.summary: Returns true when Rhino is allowed to save, false otherwise Conditions where Rhino is not allowed to save are: when evaluation licenses are expired; when a Cloud Zoo lease is expired; when a license is shared by a single user on multiple computers, and the current computer is not active.

properties.name: CheckNewObject
properties.summary: Returns true if Rhino will validate each object added to the document. This can be time consuming but is valuable for debugging.

properties.name: CommandHistoryWindowText
properties.summary: Text in Rhino's command history window.

properties.name: CommandLineOut
properties.summary: Provides a TextWriter that can write to the command line.

properties.name: CommandPrompt
properties.summary: Rhino command prompt.

properties.name: CommandWindowCaptureEnabled
properties.summary: Enable or disable capturing of the strings sent to the CommandWindow through Write and WriteLine calls

properties.name: CurrentRhinoId
properties.summary: Gets the current ID of Rhino.

properties.name: DaysUntilExpiration
properties.summary: Returns number of days until license expires. Zero when license is expired. Raises InvalidLicenseTypeException if LicenseExpires would return false.

properties.name: DefaultUiFont
properties.summary: Default font used to render user interface

properties.name: ExeServiceRelease
properties.summary: Service release version of Rhino executable (0, 1, 2, ...) The integer is the service release number of Rhino. For example, this function returns "0" if Rhino V4SR0 is running and returns "1" if Rhino V4SR1 is running.

properties.name: ExeVersion
properties.summary: Major version of Rhino executable 4, 5, ...

properties.name: InCommand
properties.summary: Gets the nested command count.

properties.name: InstallationType
properties.summary: Gets the product installation type, as seen in Rhino's ABOUT dialog box.

properties.name: InstallationTypeString
properties.summary: Gets the type of installation (product edition) of the license or lease.

properties.name: InvokeRequired
properties.summary: Returns true if we are currently not running on the main user interface thread

properties.name: IsClosing
properties.summary: Returns true if Rhino is in the process of closing, false otherwise. This can be true even before the Closing event fires, such as when RhinoDoc.CloseDocument event is called.

properties.name: IsCloudZooNode
properties.summary: Returns true if rhino is currently using the Cloud Zoo false otherwise

properties.name: IsEvaluation
properties.summary: Returns true if Rhino is licensed as an Evaluation product false otherwise

properties.name: IsExiting
properties.summary: Returns true if Rhino is in the process of exiting, false otherwise. This can be true even before the Closing event fires, such as when RhinoDoc.CloseDocument event is called.

properties.name: IsInternetAccessAllowed
properties.summary: Returns true when Rhino is allowed to access the Internet, false otherwise. Note, this does not test if Internet access is available.

properties.name: IsLicenseValidated
properties.summary: Returns true if the license is validated false otherwise

properties.name: IsOnMainThread
properties.summary: Is the current thread the main thread

properties.name: IsParentDesktop
properties.summary: Returns true if Rhino's parent window is the desktop, false otherwise.
properties.remarks: The parent of a top-level window is the desktop window. Some applications like to re-parent Rhino's main window to a window they provide. Use this property to determine if Rhino is in such a condition. Note, this property is only valid on Windows.

properties.name: IsPreRelease
properties.summary: Returns true if Rhino is compiled as pre-release build (Beta, WIP) false otherwise

properties.name: IsRunningAutomated
properties.summary: Is Rhino currently being executed through automation

properties.name: IsRunningHeadless
properties.summary: Is Rhino currently being executed in headless mode

properties.name: IsSafeModeEnabled
properties.summary: Is Rhino being executed in safe mode

properties.name: IsSkinned
properties.summary: Is Rhino currently using custom, user-interface Skin.

properties.name: LicenseExpires
properties.summary: Returns true if the license will expire false otherwise

properties.name: LicenseUserName
properties.summary: Gets the name of the user that owns the license or lease.

properties.name: LicenseUserOrganization
properties.summary: Gets the name of the organization of the user that owns the license or lease.

properties.name: LoggedInUserAvatar
properties.summary: Returns the logged in user's avatar picture. Returns a default avatar if the user does not have an avatar or if the avatar could not be fetched.

properties.name: LoggedInUserName
properties.summary: Returns the name of the logged in user, or null if the user is not logged in.

properties.name: Name
properties.summary: Gets the application name.

properties.name: NodeType
properties.summary: Gets license the node type.

properties.name: Rhino2Id
properties.summary: Gets the ID of Rhino 2.

properties.name: Rhino3Id
properties.summary: Gets the ID of Rhino 3.

properties.name: Rhino4Id
properties.summary: Gets the ID of Rhino 4.

properties.name: Rhino5Id
properties.summary: Gets the ID of Rhino 5

properties.name: Rhino6Id
properties.summary: Gets the ID of Rhino 6

properties.name: Rhino7Id
properties.summary: Gets the ID of Rhino 7

properties.name: SchemeName
properties.summary: Gets the current Registry scheme name.

properties.name: SdkServiceRelease
properties.summary: Rhino SDK 9 digit SDK service release number in the form YYYYMMDDn Service release of the Rhino SDK supported by this executable. Rhino will only load plug-ins that require a service release of <= this release number. For example, SR1 will load all plug-ins made with any SDK released up through and including the SR1 SDK. But, SR1 will not load a plug-in built using the SR2 SDK. If an "old" Rhino tries to load a "new" plug-in, the user is told that they have to get a free Rhino.exe update in order for the plug-in to load. Rhino.exe updates are available from http://www.rhino3d.com.

properties.name: SdkVersion
properties.summary: Rhino SDK 9 digit SDK version number in the form YYYYMMDDn Rhino will only load plug-ins that were build with exactly the same version of the SDK.

properties.name: SendWriteToConsole
properties.summary: Enable or disable sending command window strings to the console RhinoApp.Write(...) calls would be sent to the console when this is enabled

properties.name: SerialNumber
properties.summary: Gets the product serial number, as seen in Rhino's ABOUT dialog box.

properties.name: ToolbarFiles
properties.summary: Collection of currently open toolbar files in the application

properties.name: UpdatesAndStatisticsStatus
properties.summary: Returns true when Rhino is allowed to access the Internet, false otherwise. Note, this does not test if Internet access is available.

properties.name: UserIsLoggedIn
properties.summary: Returns true if the user is logged in; else returns false. A logged in user does not guarantee that the auth tokens managed by the CloudZooManager instance are valid.

properties.name: ValidationGracePeriodDaysLeft
properties.summary: Returns number of days within which validation must occur. Zero when validation grace period has expired. Raises InvalidLicenseTypeException if LicenseType is one of: EvaluationSaveLimited EvaluationTimeLimited Viewer Unknown

properties.name: Version
properties.summary: File version of the main Rhino process

properties.name: VersionControlRevision
properties.summary: McNeel version control revision identifier at the time this version of Rhino was built.

events.name: AppSettingsChanged
events.summary: Is raised when settings are changed.

events.name: Closing
events.summary: Is raised when the application is about to close.

events.name: EscapeKeyPressed
events.summary: Can add or removed delegates that are raised when the escape key is clicked.

events.name: Idle
events.summary: Occurs when the application finishes processing and is about to enter the idle state

events.name: Initialized
events.summary: Is raised when the application is fully initialized.

events.name: KeyboardEvent
events.summary: Can add or removed delegates that are raised by a keyboard event.

events.name: LicenseStateChanged
events.summary: Fires when the license state has changed

events.name: MainLoop
events.summary: Gets called every loop iteration inside Rhino's main message loop.

events.name: RdkCacheImageChanged
events.summary: Monitors when the RDK thumbnail cache images are changed.

events.name: RdkGlobalSettingsChanged
events.summary: Monitors when RDK global settings are modified.

events.name: RdkNewDocument
events.summary: Monitors when RDK document information is rebuilt.

events.name: RdkPlugInUnloading
events.summary: Monitors when RDK client plug-ins are unloaded.

events.name: RdkUpdateAllPreviews
events.summary: Monitors when RDK thumbnails are updated.

events.name: RendererChanged
events.summary: Monitors when Rhino's current renderer changes.
